have h₁ : φ₀'' = (fun (z : ℂ) : ℂ => (z - z.conj) / (2 * z)) := by
    funext z
    simp [φ₀'', Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> linarith
  rw [h₁]
  -- We need to show that the function z ↦ (z - conj z) / (2 * z) is holomorphic on the upper half-plane.
  -- This function is the same as z ↦ (2i Im(z)) / (2z) = i Im(z) / z.
  -- However, since conj is not holomorphic, we instead consider the function directly.
  have h₂ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun (z : ℂ) : ℂ => (z - z.conj) / (2 * z)) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
    -- Use the fact that z ↦ z - conj(z) is differentiable and z ↦ 2z is differentiable and non-zero on the upper half-plane.
    have h₃ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun (z : ℂ) : ℂ => z - z.conj) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
      -- The function z ↦ z - conj(z) is anti-holomorphic, hence differentiable as a complex function.
      have h₄ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun (z : ℂ) : ℂ => z - z.conj) ℂ := by
        -- z ↦ z is holomorphic and z ↦ conj(z) is anti-holomorphic.
        apply DifferentiableOn.sub
        · exact differentiableOn_id
        · apply DifferentiableOn.conj
          exact differentiableOn_id
      exact h₄.mono (by intro z hz; simp_all [upperHalfPlaneSet])
    have h₄ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun (z : ℂ) : ℂ => (2 : ℂ) * z) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
      -- The function z ↦ 2z is holomorphic.
      apply DifferentiableOn.const_mul
      exact differentiableOn_id.mono (by intro z hz; simp_all [upperHalfPlaneSet])
    -- Use the fact that the quotient of differentiable functions is differentiable when the denominator is non-zero.
    have h₅ : ∀ z ∈ upperHalfPlaneSet, (2 : ℂ) * z ≠ 0 := by
      intro z hz
      have h₆ : z.im > 0 := by simp_all [upperHalfPlaneSet]
      have h₇ : z.re = z.re := rfl
      have h₈ : z.im = z.im := rfl
      simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try nlinarith) <;>
      (try
        {
          contrapose! h₆
          simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
          <;> nlinarith
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          contrapose! h₆
          simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]
          <;> nlinarith
        })
    -- Apply the quotient rule for differentiability.
    have h₆ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun (z : ℂ) : ℂ => (z - z.conj) / (2 * z)) upperHalfPlaneSet := by
      apply DifferentiableOn.div h₃ h₄
      intro z hz
      exact h₅ z hz
    exact h₆
  exact h₂

    -- Use the fact that π is constant and the complex exponential function is holomorphic
    have h₁ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun z : ℂ => (π : ℂ) * (z * Complex.I)) ℍ₀ := by

      · exact differentiableOn_const _
      · apply DifferentiableOn.mul
        · exact differentiableOn_id
        · exact differentiableOn_const _
    -- The exponential function is holomorphic

      apply Complex.differentiableon_exp.comp
      apply DifferentiableOn.mul
      · exact differentiableOn_id
      · exact differentiableOn_const _
    -- The product of two holomorphic functions is holomorphic
    have h₃ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun z : ℂ => (π : ℂ) * (z * Complex.I)) ℍ₀ := h₁
    have h₄ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (z * Complex.I)) ℍ₀ := h₂
    have h₅ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun z : ℂ => (π : ℂ) * Complex.exp (z * Complex.I)) ℍ₀ := by
      apply DifferentiableOn.mul
      · exact differentiableOn_const _
      · exact h₂
    -- Use the fact that π * exp(z * I) is holomorphic and the properties of holomorphic functions
    have h₆ : DifferentiableOn ℂ (fun z : ℂ => ((π : ℂ) * Complex.exp (z * Complex.I))⁻¹) ℍ₀ := by
      apply DifferentiableOn.inv₀
      · exact h₅
      · intro z hz
        have h₇ : (π : ℂ) * Complex.exp (z * Complex.I) ≠ 0 := by
          -- π is not zero and exp(z * I) is never zero

          have h₉ : Complex.exp (z * Complex.I) ≠ 0 := Complex.exp_ne_zero _
          exact mul_ne_zero h₈ h₉
        exact h₇
    -- Since φ₀'' is the inverse of π * exp(z * I), it is holomorphic on the upper half-plane
    have h₇ : (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) = (fun z : ℂ => ((π : ℂ) * Complex.exp (z * Complex.I))⁻¹) := by

      -- Verify that φ₀'' is indeed the inverse of π * exp(z * I)

      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.I_mul_I]
      <;> field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im]
      <;> norm_num
      <;> linarith [Real.pi_pos]
    rw [h₇]
    exact h₆
  exact h
